Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“About 3 years ago, I felt depressive and nervous. And this tension last for a long time so I decided to change my situation. So, I tried to bungee jumping and this activity because in the daily life, I like some exciting sport like roller coaster and pirate ship. However, I never try to bungee jumping because it need many courage to do it. When I got the spot of bungee jumping, I find it was higher than I thought. Then, when I walked step by step to the bugee jumping table, I can even hear my heart. Although I was afraid, I felt very exciting when I was jumping because I could saw all the scenery under my feet. And at that moment, I felt I was a bird. I felt this experience get me a memorable impact because it is my first time to do it. And when I do it, I felt chilled out and unwind.”


Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Describe a time that something changed your life in good ways
You should say:

When and where it happened

What happened

How you felt about it

And explain how it changed your life in good ways”

Approximately three years ago, I experienced a profound period of depression and anxiety. This emotional turmoil persisted for an extended duration, prompting me to seek a change in my circumstances. In pursuit of exhilaration, akin to the joy I derive from engaging in thrilling amusement park rides like roller coasters and pirate ships, I ventured into the realm of bungee jumping. Despite my fondness for adrenaline-pumping activities, bungee jumping represented an uncharted territory for me, requiring a significant amount of courage to attempt.

Upon arriving at the bungee jumping site, I was taken aback by the sheer height of the jump, far exceeding my expectations. With each step towards the platform, the sound of my heartbeat became increasingly pronounced, encapsulating my escalating fear. Yet, as I made the leap, my fear was immediately supplanted by an indescribable sense of exhilaration. Soaring through the air, I was greeted with breathtaking views unfolding beneath my feet, momentarily embodying the freedom of a bird in flight.

This experience not only offered me a fleeting escape from my fears but also imparted a lasting impact on me. For the first time in my life, I had confronted and overcome a formidable challenge head-on. Completing the jump instilled in me a newfound sense of relaxation and an ability to unwind from the stresses of daily life.

Reflecting on this transformative event, I realize it marked a pivotal moment in my journey towards emotional resilience and self-discovery. It taught me the value of stepping out of my comfort zone and facing my fears, thereby fostering a greater sense of confidence and adventure in my approach to life. This shift in perspective has had a positive effect on my overall well-being, encouraging me to embrace challenges with open arms and an open heart.
Thank you. Please use some simpler words.

Three years ago, I was feeling really down and anxious for a long time. I knew I needed to do something different to feel better. I liked thrilling rides like roller coasters, so I decided to try bungee jumping, even though it seemed really scary because it takes a lot of bravery. When I got to the place where you jump from, it was way higher than I thought, which made me even more scared. But as I walked up to the spot to jump, my heart was racing.

Despite my fear, jumping was amazing. I felt like I was flying and could see everything below me. It was like being a bird for a little while. This jump was a big deal for me because it was my first time doing something so brave. After I did it, I felt much more relaxed and free from worry.

This bungee jump changed my life in good ways. It showed me that I could face my fears and do exciting things I never thought I could. It made me more confident and happy to try new challenges. Now, I feel stronger and ready to take on tough things in life, knowing that I can handle them.

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below. Use simple words like the above one:
“For this topic, I am going to talk about a difficult task that I help my friends to improve their math grades. About three years ago, my friend failed the mathematical exam and they need to reach the passing score. Otherwise, they need to retake the course. So, we make a schedule to help them first and foremost, listen carefully in class, finish the teacher's homework carefully every day. In addition, review new content in time to form a good habit. Last but not least, prepare a wrong problem collection if they make some difficult difficult contact they need to write down. I think the task was difficult for me because although I am good at mathematics, but I have no experience to teach others. I think I am not suitable to teach my friends because it's important for them. Likely, in the end they got a good score and they all pass the exam.”

Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Describe a difficult task that you completed at work/study that you felt proud of
You should say:

What the task was

How you completed it

Why the task was difficult

Why you were proud of the completion of the task”

"I want to share about a tough challenge where I helped my friends improve their math grades. Three years ago, one of my friends failed their math exam and had to pass it or they'd have to retake the whole course. We decided to tackle this together. Here's what we did: we set up a study schedule, made sure to listen attentively in class, diligently completed all homework daily, and regularly reviewed new material to build good study habits. We also created a list of problems they found hard to solve, so we could go over them together.

The task was tough for me because, even though I'm good at math, I had never taught anyone before. I doubted if I was the right person to help, considering how crucial it was for them. But in the end, they all improved their scores and passed the exam. 

I felt proud because it showed me that with dedication and the right strategies, I could help others succeed, even in areas I found challenging. It wasn't just about the math; it was about learning to teach and communicate effectively, and seeing my friends succeed was truly rewarding."

“Do you prefer to wear comfortable and casual clothes or smart clothes?”

"For this question, I prefer wearing comfortable and casual clothes, both when I'm attending school and going to the gym. I believe that dressing nicely doesn't necessarily offer any advantages in a school setting, and comfortable attire allows for easier movement during exercise at the gym. Wearing casual clothes to school suits me best."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“Public facilities Particularly like parks are wearing for for old people in the community because for a young young teenager or middle-aged people who can go anywhere for doing sports but for some old people in the community they can't they can they don't have a really good movement they can't really what was the move to move to the hands go to a place toolbar to get some exercise so they need to go to some place that earned that and they they need a place to do some easy exercise but not hard but still has some exercise so mmm I think the environment that the parks provide are good for community activity especially for these old people who wants to pull on to get exercise”

Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“What are the benefits of public facilities?”

"Public facilities, particularly parks, serve as essential spaces for elderly members of the community. Unlike younger individuals or middle-aged people who can easily travel to various locations for sports or exercise, some elderly people may have limited mobility and require nearby options for physical activity. Parks provide a suitable and accessible environment for them to engage in gentle exercises that are easy to perform but still beneficial for their health. Thus, the welcoming and inclusive atmosphere of parks is especially advantageous for older adults seeking to maintain an active lifestyle."

“I think and seeing the Huawei that the subway is popular is because sometimes they are some traffic some some traffic happens on the role and sometimes just getting slower and slower but the subway the train doesn't affect by these things these elements so the time the subway use is out always the same and it won't become slower because the piho anymore but the normal Road will be well be lowers be lower speed just because the subway and just because they are too much on the road”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Why are some public transport methods popular, such as the subway?“

"I believe one reason the subway is popular is that traffic congestion on the roads can slow down travel, whereas the subway is unaffected by these issues. The subway maintains a consistent travel time regardless of road conditions, making it a reliable mode of transportation. In contrast, travel by road can often be delayed due to heavy traffic. This efficiency and reliability make the subway a preferred choice for many commuters."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“Some public transport options are less popular because of freaking delays and overcrowding delays our major haddock especially during rush hours turning a short commute into a long or they'll overcome our crowding is another issue is uncomfortable and sometimes feels unsafe when you are packed like startling lens all of us or trained with little personal space”

“Why are some public transport methods unpopular?”

"Some public transport options are unpopular due to frequent delays and overcrowding. Delays are a significant headache, particularly during rush hours when what should be a short commute becomes much longer. Overcrowding is another critical issue; it is not only uncomfortable but can also feel unsafe as passengers are packed tightly together, leaving little personal space. These factors contribute to the unpopularity of certain public transport methods."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“People often link towards reliable public transports options they appreciate the inpati and protectively of a good public transport system like the subway that never delay and it's actually Urban people who live in urban areas for the elderly prefer to some easy to use we hose like some public and comfort and easy to access and I think these are most important points for oto so they often chose cars that are single to operate or some public bus”

“What kind of transport do young people and old people prefer?”

"People often gravitate towards reliable public transport options, valuing the punctuality and efficiency of systems like the subway, which rarely experiences delays. Urban residents, including the elderly, prefer transport methods that are convenient, comfortable, and easy to access. These criteria are essential, leading them to choose vehicles that are simple to navigate, such as certain cars or public buses, tailored to their needs. These preferences highlight the importance of accessibility and reliability in public transport for both young and old users."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“If the restaurant just make makes a small mistake, I will bear because I think it can save my time. However, if I encounter some worse service, I would express my discontent to the manager of the restaurant. If they can't solve the problem for me, I would call the relevant department to help.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“How would you react if you received poor customer service at a restaurant?”

"If a restaurant makes a minor mistake, I am willing to overlook it as I believe it saves time. However, if I experience significantly poor service, I would voice my dissatisfaction to the restaurant's manager. Should they fail to resolve the issue to my satisfaction, I would then consider contacting the appropriate authority for assistance."
Come up with 2 idioms for the above revised version and identify them.

1. "Overlook it" - an idiom meaning to ignore or disregard a minor problem or mistake.
2. "Voice my dissatisfaction" - while not a classic idiom, this phrase is a common way to express the action of speaking out when unhappy with a situation, using 'voice' metaphorically to mean 'to express.'

As older people are more likely to choose forgive other people's mistakes while younger people may feel angry and irritated so they make complaints to express their dissatisfaction. And younger people know more ways to complain by using the Internet.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Who are more likely to make complaints, older people or younger people?”

"I believe younger people are more prone to lodging complaints compared to older individuals. Older people often opt to forgive mistakes, whereas younger people might feel angered or annoyed, leading them to express their dissatisfaction through complaints. Furthermore, younger individuals are more adept at using the internet to voice their grievances."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“I suppose the careless people are prone to lose items than others because they are more forget the item position and they are and they always like the routine or lack of they don't have a custom to watch their thing organized organizing. So, I think this kind of people tend to lose things more easily.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“What kind of people tend to lose things more often than others?”

"I believe that careless individuals are more likely to lose items than others because they often forget where they've placed things and lack a routine or habit of keeping their belongings organized. Therefore, people with these tendencies tend to lose things more easily."
Make the above revised answer a bit more lengthier. Come up with two more sentences.

"I believe that careless individuals are more likely to lose items than others because they often forget where they've placed things and lack a routine or habit of keeping their belongings organized. This lack of attention to where items are left and a general disorganization means that when it comes time to find something, they struggle to recall its last known location. Additionally, their tendency not to maintain a systematic approach to storing items contributes to their propensity for misplacing things. Therefore, people with these tendencies tend to lose things more easily, as their habits do not support the careful tracking and organizing of their personal belongings."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“I suppose the items which are not used frequently are more lead to be forget because in our daily life people will not pay more attention to these items so we are tend to forget the location of the things. And in the meantime, we will also lost the frequently use things because when we feel tired or exhausted, we have no effort to pay attention to the important items. As a result, we lost these things.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“What kind of things do people often lose?”

"I believe that items not used regularly are more likely to be forgotten because, in our daily lives, we tend not to pay much attention to them, making it easy to forget where they are placed. Likewise, items we use frequently can also be lost, particularly when we are tired or exhausted and lack the energy to keep track of important belongings. Consequently, it is during these times, whether due to infrequent use or exhaustion, that we tend to lose things."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:

Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Why do some people find lost things easier than others?”

"I believe that some people might be more careless than others or may lack the good habit of keeping tabs on their belongings. In contrast, there are individuals who naturally pay more attention to their items, or consistently check on their belongings to ensure they are not forgotten."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“I think these kinds of people are more likely lack a sense of morality. So, they so they pick up the lost thing and sell them for money. Or they use these things because they are attracted by these items so they not willing to return these things to other people. In summary, I think this kind of behaviors are not good. We should return the lost things to other people.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“What do you think are the reasons why some pepoe pick up lost things and don’t give them back?”

"I believe that individuals who fail to return lost items may often lack a sense of morality. As a result, they may choose to sell these found items for money, or keep them due to an attraction to the items, showing a reluctance to return them to their rightful owners. In summary, I consider such behavior to be unethical. It is important that we strive to return lost items to their owners."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“Certainly yes. I consider it's duty for parents to teach their kids to return the lost items because parents should teach their people to be an honest person so all of them have responsibilities to return the lost thing and when we lose some belongings, someone can return them to us.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Should parents teach their children to return things lost by others?”

"Absolutely, yes. I believe it is a parent's duty to teach their children to return lost items. Parents ought to instill honesty in their children, emphasizing that it is everyone's responsibility to return found items. This way, if we ever lose something ourselves, there's a better chance someone will return it to us."
Thank you. Add two more sentences to the revised version to make it a bit longer.

"Absolutely, yes. I believe it is a parent's duty to teach their children to return lost items. Parents ought to instill honesty in their children, emphasizing that it is everyone's responsibility to return found items. By doing so, they foster a society based on trust and mutual respect. Furthermore, instilling such values from a young age ensures that children grow up understanding the importance of kindness and civic duty. This way, if we ever lose something ourselves, there's a better chance someone will return it to us."

Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below. Expand those two points in 3 sentences:
“Listening actively and providing an enjoyable atmosphere are both essential to motivate children to express themselves when they are facing questions they are not willing to answer. “

"Active listening plays a crucial role in encouraging children to open up, especially when they are hesitant to answer certain questions. It shows them that their thoughts and feelings are valued, which can significantly boost their confidence. Similarly, creating an enjoyable atmosphere fosters a safe and comfortable environment for them, making it easier for them to express themselves without fear or reluctance."

In contrast, when uh uh when people are in business social situation, it's better to it's not suitable to be talkative since it may lead the listerns uncomfortable and exhausted. Therefore, it varies from person to person as well as from setting to setting.”

"It varies depending on the occasion. When interacting with friends or family, engaging in conversation allows individuals to share daily anecdotes and strengthen their bonds. However, in a business or formal social situation, being overly talkative might not be appropriate, as it could make listeners feel uncomfortable or overwhelmed. Thus, the appropriateness of being talkative depends both on the individual and the context."

Moreover, overtalk may also disrupt distract other student's attention. In conclusion, it's essential to keep more quiet when children are in a training session.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:

"During training classes, it's important for children to speak less so they can concentrate fully on the teacher's instructions and reflect thoughtfully instead of talking excessively. Excessive talking can also distract the attention of other students. Therefore, maintaining quieter behavior is crucial when children are participating in a training session."

Being a problem-solver who interacts across various departments, having strong communication skills and a willingness to engage with different people is crucial in finding win-win solutions benefitting all functions within the company. Therefore, financial management is one of the careers that immediately comes to mind when thinking about jobs that necessitate talkativeness."
